titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 5965

p.seg: To all Christ's faithful to whom these present letters shall have come, Simon son of Elias of Prenderguest sends greeting.

to have given, granted, and by this my present charter to have made firm, for myself and for my heirs for ever, to God and to the blessed Cuthbert and the blessed Æbbe of Coldingham, and to the monks in that place serving God now and in the future, for the salvatino of my soul and of my ancestors', and in return for a certain sum of money which the prior and convent of Coldingham paid to me as a downpayment in my need, ten acres of land in the ploughland at Coteflat with one toft and croft of one acre of land on the western side of my brother Richard's garden, which the same Richard gave to me in return for my homage and service; for the said monks and their successrs to hold and to have as freely and quitely as I held the aforesaid land at any time. And I, Simon, and my heirs shall warrant the aforesaid land to the aforesaid monks against all men, with all its pertinents inside and outside the toun, without any withholding.
And so that this my donation and confirmation should possess the perpetual strength of a charter, I have applied my seal to this charter.

With these witnesses, the lords David of Graham, then sheriff of Berwick, David of Haddington, Ralph Noble, Alan of Harcarse, William of Scremerston, Adam of Morham, Robert of Paxton, Adam son of John of Ayton, Adam son of Walter of the same, Patrick Scot, Patrick son of Colman of Oldcambus, and others.
